title: Emotional Intelligence (EQ) Test
description: Measure how well you recognize, manage, and use emotions in daily life. Choose the answer that best describes you.

questions.question: When your friend gets angry for no apparent reason, you...

options.text: Listen and try to understand why
Get angry too because you feel disrespected
Avoid them until they calm down on their own
Blame them for ruining the mood

questions.question: You fail an important exam. Your first reaction...

options.text: Disappointed, then immediately think of how to improve
Get angry at yourself and others
Feel worthless and want to give up
Don't care, because you think it's not important

questions.question: When two of your friends are arguing, you...

options.text: Listen to both sides and help find a solution
Take the side you think is right
Leave them alone to avoid getting involved
Join the argument to make it more exciting

questions.question: When receiving harsh criticism from your boss...

options.text: Accept it calmly and look for lessons
Defend yourself with long excuses
Feel hurt and find it hard to continue working
Ignore the criticism completely

questions.question: You see someone crying alone in a public place...

options.text: Approach them and offer help
Glance briefly then walk away
Feel uncomfortable and move away
Take a photo to post on social media

questions.question: Vacation plans change completely due to a friend's unilateral decision...

options.text: Disappointed, but try to adjust and enjoy it
Get angry and blame the friend
Stay silent and not enjoy the vacation at all
Go home immediately and cancel the vacation

questions.question: How often do you notice changes in your mood?

options.text: Quite often, I know the triggers
Sometimes, but I don't pay much attention
Rarely, I focus more on other things
Almost never, emotions just come and go

questions.question: Someone cancels an appointment without notice...

options.text: Annoyed, but immediately find another activity
Very angry and confront that person
Sad and feel unappreciated all day
Don't care, because you didn't want to meet anyway

questions.question: You prefer to resolve conflicts by...

options.text: Talking honestly and finding a solution together
Staying silent and hoping time resolves it
Blaming the other party until they give in
Ending the relationship immediately

questions.question: When you see a friend succeed, you...

options.text: Feel happy and motivated
Feel envious and compare yourself
Feel indifferent, don't care
Feel inferior

questions.question: Work/college tasks pile up and deadlines are approaching...

options.text: Set priorities and do them one by one
Get angry and complain to everyone
Panic to the point of crying and wanting to give up
Don't care and instead play games/sleep

questions.question: A colleague gets a promotion you were aiming for...

options.text: Congratulate them and reflect on my own shortcomings
Look for their faults behind their back
Feel very stupid and lose work motivation
Don't care, think the system is indeed unfair

questions.question: You make a big mistake at work...

options.text: Immediately report, apologize, and bring a solution
Cover up the mistake and blame the situation
Avoid your boss because you're too scared
Don't care, the company won't go bankrupt anyway

questions.question: There's a team member who is always passive...

options.text: Talk to them nicely to find out their obstacles
Scold them in front of other members
Do their part yourself while feeling resentful
Remove their name from the task without warning

questions.question: A new rule is suddenly imposed and burdens you...

options.text: Find out the reason and try to adapt
Protest immediately with a loud tone
Accept it and feel like a victim
Intentionally break the rule

questions.question: Your partner or close friend criticizes your bad habit...

options.text: Listen openly and try to change
Retaliate by criticizing their bad habit
Sulk and ignore them for days
Tell them to find another friend/partner

questions.question: You experience rejection (love, job, or project)...

options.text: Accept it gracefully and move on
Badmouth the party that rejected you
Isolate yourself for days feeling worthless
Act arrogantly as if you don't need it

questions.question: Someone keeps interrupting while you speak...

options.text: Politely ask them to let you finish
Yell at them to be quiet
Stop talking altogether and feel annoyed alone
Raise your voice too to drown theirs out

questions.question: Dealing with an angry customer or stranger...

options.text: Stay calm and find the core of the problem
Get emotional too and argue back
Confused, trembling, and don't know what to do
Leave them talking to themselves

questions.question: Disagreeing on politics or principles with a friend...

options.text: Respect the differences without having to agree
Argue fiercely until they give in
Slowly distance yourself because you feel incompatible
Intentionally mock their views continuously

questions.question: A friend borrows your favorite item and breaks it...

options.text: Gently reprimand them and ask for accountability
Rage and break their belongings too
Cry silently but say 'it's okay'
Sarcastically hint at them continuously on social media

questions.question: You receive great praise in public...

options.text: Say thank you humbly
Be arrogant and feel the greatest
Feel very embarrassed and deny the praise
Think the praise is just fake pleasantry

questions.question: Seeing a friend bullied or mocked in a group chat...

options.text: Defend the friend or reprimand in the group
Join in the laughter or add more mockery
Stay silent because you're afraid of being bullied too
Leave the group without explanation

questions.question: Losing an important item due to your own carelessness...

options.text: Accept the reality and try to search logically
Rage and throw things around
Blame yourself excessively all day
Look for a scapegoat to blame

questions.question: Waiting in a long line and someone cuts in front...

options.text: Reprimand them firmly but politely
Immediately yell and pull that person
Stay silent and suppress your annoyance inside
Intentionally push that person from behind

questions.question: Feeling very anxious before a big presentation...

options.text: Do breathing techniques and focus on the material
Vent your nervousness by yelling at friends
So scared you want to fake being sick
Go ahead unprepared because you underestimate it

questions.question: Hearing bad rumors about yourself at work/campus...

options.text: Find the source and clarify calmly
Confront the people talking about you
Cry and refuse to go to work/college
Start a counter-rumor about them

questions.question: You have to give negative feedback to a teammate...

options.text: Deliver it professionally and supportively
Speak harshly so they learn
Don't reprimand because you feel bad
Ask someone else to deliver it

questions.question: A friend hasn't repaid a debt, but they post about buying luxury items...

options.text: Ask them directly and firmly
Rant in the comments on their social media
Only feel annoyed inside but dare not ask
Post sarcastic statuses without tagging them

questions.question: Being in a new environment and not knowing anyone yet...

options.text: Take the initiative to smile and greet first
Act arrogant to be respected
Hide in a corner and play on your phone continuously
Wait for someone to worship/greet you first

questions.question: After working hard, your efforts are underappreciated...

options.text: Know your own worth even without external validation
Get angry and threaten to quit
Feel very sad and start slacking off
Sabotage others' work to stand out

questions.question: Reprimanded by your boss/lecturer in front of many people...

options.text: Restrain yourself, apologize, then evaluate later
Talk back and argue right there
Cry on the spot because you're so embarrassed
Roll your eyes and act indifferent

questions.question: Someone else deliberately takes credit for your brilliant idea...

options.text: Clarify the facts professionally to the decision maker
Rage and curse them in front of everyone
Stay silent, resigned to being a victim
Take them down secretly later

questions.question: Stuck in a massive traffic jam while in a hurry...

options.text: Take a deep breath, notify your delay, and accept it
Honk continuously and curse
Feel very stressed, panic, and want to cry
Recklessly overtake dangerously

questions.question: Facing repeated failures on the same thing...

options.text: Analyze new strategies not yet tried
Get angry at fate and feel the world is unfair
Give up completely, convinced you're stupid
Look for someone else to blame for the failure

questions.question: Having a very bad day (everything goes wrong)...

options.text: Give yourself time to rest for a while
Vent your anger on family/friends
Feel endless self-pity
Get drunk or do destructive things

questions.question: Someone is crying hysterically asking for help near you...

options.text: Stay calm and ask what help they need
Tell them to be quiet because they're noisy
Panic too, get confused, and cry as well
Just record them from afar without helping

questions.question: Being on a team whose work principles don't align with yours...

options.text: Compromise on technicalities while maintaining integrity
Force everyone to follow your way
Be passive, silently follow the flow to stay safe
Intentionally slow down the work to make the team fail

questions.question: Realizing you have been very rude to a friend...

options.text: Apologize sincerely without many excuses
Deny it and think they're too sensitive
Feel embarrassed and intentionally avoid them forever
Don't care, think it's normal

questions.question: Seeing a colleague struggling while you have many tasks yourself...

options.text: Offer small help or advice without sacrificing my priorities
Mock them for being slow
Drop all my tasks to help them completely
Pretend not to see so I'm not asked to help

questions.question: Facing very sensitive/personal questions at a family gathering...

options.text: Answer diplomatically or deflect with a smile
Get angry and yell at the family member who asked
Feel sad, pressured, then go to your room
Retaliate by asking about their shameful secrets

questions.question: Hearing complaints from many friends at once...

options.text: Be a good listener but maintain your energy boundaries
Tell them all to stop complaining
Get swept up in their negative emotions until you're stressed too
Ignore them, pretend to listen while playing on your phone

questions.question: Being excluded or not invited to a group hangout...

options.text: Don't care, find positive activities yourself
Confront them about why you weren't invited
Feel very depressed and feel like you have no friends
Badmouth their event elsewhere

questions.question: Someone keeps bringing up your past mistakes...

options.text: Assert your boundary that the past is over
Rage because you can't stand being reminded
Cry because you still feel guilty
Retaliate by exposing their past

questions.question: Facing a breakup with a close friend/partner...

options.text: Process the sadness appropriately and move on with life
Blame them entirely for the breakup
Be shattered for months without wanting to move
Immediately look for a new distraction the next day

questions.question: An emergency occurs (minor accident, small fire)...

options.text: Act quickly, control emotions, and look for a solution
Rage and blame people around
Panic, scream hysterically without doing anything
Run to save yourself, leave others behind

questions.question: Given a task outside your main responsibility...

options.text: Professionally discuss capacity and compensation
Immediately refuse curtly
Reluctantly accept while grumbling behind the scenes
Accept but do it carelessly

questions.question: Seeing someone win by cheating...

options.text: Report them with solid evidence
Physically attack them directly
Accept it, think the world is indeed dirty
Start cheating too next time

questions.question: Realizing you are burned out (severe emotional exhaustion)...

options.text: Ask for leave, rest, and dare to say 'no'
Vent curses at the workplace
Withdraw socially and feel numb
Force yourself to keep working while taking medication

questions.question: Your main goal in studying emotional intelligence is...

options.text: To understand myself and respond to situations more wisely
To be able to manipulate others
To appear psychologically smart
Just for fun, taking a test
